item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This honestly feels like wasted potential, having this just be a thing what makes you go crazy.

There's a genuinely interesting idea in an object that teases patterns of behavior that might not actually be there, with researchers trying to figure out what made it start emitting gamma radiation for 10 minutes radiation last Tuesday (determined to be someone whistling Stairway To Heaven in the adjacent hallway) or what triggers its periodic bouts of rapid spinning (analysis suggests possible relations to a particular ratio of stock values for several companies based out of Japan and/or the average rate of coffee consumption in New York City)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why did I never vote on this before, this is amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting. I feel like this one kinda… fell apart on further analysis for me.

+ show block
– hide block

I would definitely say this moved me during the actual process of reading it. I might have even felt like I was having fun! But, reading back, stuff just starts to rub me the wrong way. Like, the lost mechanic kinda lost me.1 It could have been interesting, but was vague enough that I didn't even really feel like questioning it.

The ending was the main oddity for me. It could definitely just be my own canon biases, but I feel like the foundation would be long gone if it was this easy to get them to just forget about losing a city. If it was trying to be funny, it just made me think about too many things for me to be laughing. If it was supposed to be horrifying, it was too absurd to get me there. If it was supposed to make me think, the whole article was too vague to give me enough details to think about.

I actually think this would have benefited from going harder into either horror or humor. For horror, just continue to play it straight past where it was. Show the foundation seriously struggling, or even civilization seriously struggling. Hell, go full Broken Masquerade with it. For humor, maybe the steaks just need to be lowered. Not sure.

Footnotes
1. heh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish you'd lean much harder into conveying the fascination and suppression of critical thinking experienced by the researchers. I "got it" after reading a few of the tests, but I felt like there wasn't much more payoff from reading the rest of the article. "Too much like another article" isn't always a valid criticism, but I can't help but wonder if I personally would have been more charmed by this if I hadn't already read 2719, or 3942, and other articles that rely on the theme of obscure, yet escalating test results.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how childish some of the language sounds. It makes the compulsion and tests just seem like innocent fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers
Spioler alert

Creepy ending. Most articles I've seen like this end with the Foundation upgrading the item to Keter class and then rewriting its containment procedures. This one just ends with the O5s demanding more tests like children begging for candy.
From the Foundation's point of view, this documentation would be terrible: the scope of the cognitohazard is never investigated, with its existence left entirely unmentioned; there is no explanation of why any actions other than a change of colour are described as "SCP-4342 does something. As a result…"; and of course, the object class is entirely wrong.
But this breaks the rules in all the right ways for me as a reader.

Marvelous. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I was going to pounce on "Incredibly" as not clinical-sounding enough, but as I read on I realized that it honestly works considering the effect of the skip. Still didn't vote, though, because it seems too close to 3107 for comfort, and the inclusion of 914 and 682 just seems a bit too fanservicey. It seems too cozy for an apocalypse.

(Let me know if any of this post needs to be collapsed. I couldn't decide if anything counted as a spoiler.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoiler
- SCP-4342 spoils the joke. As a result, the joke becomes lost�0�. More test needed.

SCP-4342 has a strong memetic effect compelling researchers to perform more and more superfluous tests on it no matter the cost, and it's slowly making the entire Foundation fall apart. See SCP-3107 as a parallel.

Footnotes
1. Judged no longer recognizable or functional as funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's nice to read about an SCP that does things. More test.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I genuinely don't get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this entry does something for me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thirded. To me, this just feels like yet another "cognitohazard affects documentation and personnel" article. I've reread it twice, looking for more patterns and more depth, but I can't really find it. 4511 does this trope much better, imo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More upvote! +1

item.title: Big funny
item.content:encoded: Big funny +1 hahahahaha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It does everything but
Go inside.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. It could be that I'm missing something, but even if that's the case, I don't think the article does enough to indicate where to look. In something like SCP-2719 it's very clear what text needs to be examined further, but the esotericism here feels far more directionless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I admit to being intrigued by the 4156 mini-canon you’re putting together but that doesn’t excuse that this article is an exceedingly tired rehash of SCP-2790, SCP-4511, SCP-1561, with a splash of SCP-3005.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why did I just upvote an article with a 682 crosstest? I can't explain why I like this article as much as I do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What.

For some reason, I feel that this SCP doesn't have enough test logs. In fact, I think that the author should go ahead and add more. I'm am generally unhappy with the lack of test logs. It would benefit from more test logs.

In conclusion. More test.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Testing Personnel: Dr. Henzoid
Stimuli: Reading SCP-4342's file.

Results: Dr. Henzoid does something. As a result, SCP-4342 gains an upvote.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Kyza, Cregan_Stark, Prior, Crashington, Henzoid and DianaBerry for their feedback!

PLEASE DON’T SUE TANHONY

https://search.creativecommons.org/photos/57185f3c-2eb6-475b-b346-5d1aa872bf8c "paper cube" by ups2006 (CC BY 2.0)
